opportunities.rationale: CPI printed in-line at +2.4% YoY / +0.3% MoM — no hawkish surprise. Initial knee-jerk moves are likely to fade intraday; best edge is fading the first overreaction spike on US500 or NAS100. Gold may soften short-term on reduced safe-haven demand, but oil-driven forward inflation keeps underlying bid intact.
Labor market readings remain a key Fed input ahead of the 18-19 Mar FOMC. A meaningful claims miss could revive rate-cut chatter and lift indices; a beat reinforces the no-cut narrative.

Post-CPI FOMC is the primary risk event of the period: no cut expected but Powell's language on oil-driven inflation will drive the direction of risk assets and FX. Reduce directional exposure in the 16-19 Mar window; re-enter after the press conference clarifies tone.

opportunities.event_name: US-Iran Conflict & Strait of Hormuz Disruption
opportunities.rationale: Ongoing US-Iran military confrontation has pushed Brent to $87-92 and WTI to ~$84, a 30%+ YTD rally. Any Strait of Hormuz headline (escalation or de-escalation) can move oil, gold, and indices sharply intraday. De-risk before major headlines; trade reaction fades where spreads allow.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter long on pullback to 5025-5080 zone and RSI oversold on 1h; invalidated if daily close below 4925.
opportunities.rationale: Gold trading at $5,183 with a short-term bearish correction underway toward the 5025 support area. The bullish channel remains intact; geopolitical safe-haven demand and energy-driven inflation keep the longer-term upside toward 5445 viable. CPI in-line reduces the immediate safe-haven spike but does not reverse the macro bid.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Long on dip to 80-82 on any de-escalation dip that fails to break below 77.5; exit if diplomatic ceasefire is confirmed.
opportunities.rationale: WTI at $83.87 and Brent near $88-92 driven by Strait of Hormuz supply disruption. A pullback to the 80-82 area offers a risk-defined long with target near prior intraday highs. Thesis invalidated by confirmed ceasefire or significant OPEC supply response.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter long on intraday dip to 6720-6760 if price holds above 6700 after CPI reaction settles; avoid entering into FOMC week (16-19 Mar).
opportunities.rationale: S&P 500 near 6,798 with CPI coming in benign — short-term oversold bounce candidate. However, the FOMC meeting (18-19 Mar) caps upside for swing positions; keep holding period short and exit before 16 Mar.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter long on intraday dip to 24600-24800 only if price holds above 24200 after initial CPI reaction; stand aside if bearish momentum persists.
opportunities.rationale: NAS100 near 24,956 but off highs; AI capex fears and macro uncertainty weigh. Only trade if price action confirms buyers at key support — low conviction given FOMC proximity and elevated VIX.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter on dips toward 23500-23800 if US-Iran tensions show signs of easing; exit on re-escalation headlines.
opportunities.rationale: DAX recovered ~2.4% to ~23,935 on Trump comments hinting at Middle East de-escalation. German equities are heavily leveraged to energy prices and European export demand; any credible ceasefire catalyst could drive a sharp relief bounce toward prior highs.

US PPI on 18 Mar lands hours before the FOMC decision, making it the final read on upstream inflation. Prior monthly prints running ~0.4-0.5% MoM and ~2.9-3.0% YoY. A material upside surprise reinforces higher-for-longer Fed expectations, weighing on indices and supporting USD; a soft print eases pressure and favours a relief bid in risk assets. High conviction given FOMC co-scheduling — directional bias set at print.

opportunities.event_name: European Central Bank Rate Decision & Press Conference
opportunities.rationale: ECB meets 19 Mar in a week dense with central-bank decisions (Fed, BoC, SNB, BoE also meeting). Rates around 2.15-2.00% with a weak Eurozone growth backdrop. Market balanced between further dovish guidance and a prolonged pause. Any surprise on rate path or Lagarde tone produces outsized moves in EUR and Eurozone indices given already elevated global VIX. Reduce exposure ahead; re-enter on confirmed direction post-press conference.
